reasoning: The empirical base rate for any-increase ACLED questions is provided as 0.225. Protest events typically exhibit right-skewed distributions throughout the year, with large spikes around spring events (e.g., May Day, Newroz, Women's Day). This pushes the annual 30-day average (mean) significantly higher than the median 30-day period. Because the implicit resolution date is likely in the winter (associated with the close date of Jan 2026), colder weather and a lack of traditional anniversaries generally suppress protest turnout, making it highly probable that the 30-day period leading up to the resolution date will fall below the annual average. Without specific triggering events definitively scheduled, defaulting closely to the empirical base rate of 0.225 is the optimal strategy.
